title: cotyledon pumila
intro: Cotyledon pumila, also known as dwarf or baby jade, is a small, succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cotyledon pumila include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to support overall well-being and address specific health concerns.
